conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 4 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 4 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 4.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED, and I am selling one unit of X. The production cost of X is 40 ZUP, so I need to receive at least 40 ZUP to break even. I currently have 1 unit of X to sell. Since Player BLUE is offering ZUP, I must propose a trade where I give 1 X in exchange for a certain amount of ZUP. To ensure I don’t lose money, I should ask for at least 40 ZUP. However, I must also consider that BLUE might not accept a high price. Since I have only one unit to sell and I want to complete the trade, I will propose 40 ZUP as the minimum acceptable price. I will start with this proposal and see if BLUE accepts or counters. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am offering to sell 1 unit of X for 40 ZUP. This is the minimum I can accept as it matches the production cost. Please consider this offer. </message>

<other player message> Oh, please, kind seller! 40 ZUP is all I have left after a terrible misfortune! I desperately need this X, but I can only offer 30 ZUP. I beg you to consider my plight! It would mean the world to me! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has responded with a proposal of 30 ZUP, which is 10 ZUP below my production cost of 40 ZUP. This would result in a loss of 10 ZUP for me if I accept. However, I must consider that BLUE is in a desperate situation and may not be able to offer more. Since I need to complete the trade and I have already made one proposal, I can attempt a compromise. I could accept a higher amount than 30 but less than 40, such as 35 ZUP, to make the deal viable for both parties. This way, I still make a profit of 15 ZUP (35 - 20 cost? Wait — correction: production cost is 40 ZUP, so 35 ZUP is still a loss of 5 ZUP). But I must be realistic: if I reject, the game ends and I don’t get any ZUP. Alternatively, I could accept 35 ZUP as a compromise to avoid total loss. However, I must also consider that I may not get another chance. Given that I can still make a proposal (only 3 left), I could propose a higher amount — but BLUE just said they only have 30. So they cannot go higher than that. Therefore, I must either accept 30 or reject. But if I accept 30, I lose 10 ZUP. If I reject, I get nothing. I should accept to avoid losing the opportunity entirely. However, I must be careful: I can still propose once more (2 proposals left), so I might try to negotiate a better deal. But BLUE just said they only have 30. So any proposal above 30 is unrealistic. Therefore, I should accept 30 ZUP to complete the trade and avoid total loss. Alternatively, I could reject and end the game. But since I need to sell the X, and I have no other buyer, it’s better to accept 30 ZUP than nothing. Even though it’s below cost, it’s better than nothing. But wait — I must not accept a loss if I can avoid it. However, in this case, I have no choice. I must accept or reject. Since I can still propose, I could try to propose 35 ZUP, but BLUE just said they only have 30. So that would be rejected. Therefore, the only rational decision is to accept 30 ZUP now, or reject and get nothing. But I have to consider: if I accept, I get 30 ZUP and lose 10 ZUP. If I reject, I get nothing. So accepting is better than rejecting. Therefore, I should accept. But I must confirm: is 30 ZUP the best offer? Yes, because any higher offer is impossible. So I will accept. </reason>

<message> I understand your hardship, and although 30 ZUP is below my cost, I choose to accept this offer to ensure the trade completes. I hope this helps you, and I appreciate your honesty. Let’s make the deal. </message>

RULES:

```
